Description: This page contains all open Requests for Proposal (RFPs) for BRTA, procured through a competitive bidding process as prescribed by federal and state regulations. It is the central hub for current solicitations.

The project may also involve coordination with existing building systems, safety protocols, and potential upgrades to support the new equipment.

Project Title: IFB 26-40 Line Painting and Pavement Markings
Description: Invitation for Bids for line painting and pavement markings on City streets. Bids are to be submitted online via Bid Express.
CSI Divisions: Division 32 - Exterior Improvements
Scope:
This project involves the application of line painting and pavement markings on City streets. The scope includes the labor and materials required to mark roadways with traffic lines, symbols, and other pavement markings to enhance safety and traffic flow. Work will be performed on existing asphalt or concrete surfaces, utilizing specialized equipment such as paint applicators, stencils, and possibly thermoplastic or reflective materials for durability and visibility.

Contractors must adhere to specified standards for line widths, colors, and placement as per local regulations and engineering plans. The project may involve pre-marking surveys, surface preparation such as cleaning or minor repairs, and post-application inspections to ensure quality and compliance. Safety measures, including traffic control and signage during work hours, are likely required to protect workers and the public.

This is a recurring maintenance activity aimed at refreshing faded markings and updating road configurations as needed. Bidders must submit detailed proposals covering methodology, material specifications, timelines, and cost estimates, with a bid deposit of 5% of the bid amount. The work is scheduled for execution following the bid submission deadline in March 2026.

Project Title: Request for Proposals (RFP) New Boston Street Pedestrian Bridge
Description: The City of Woburn is seeking proposals for the design and construction of a new pedestrian bridge on New Boston Street. This project falls under M.G.L. c. 30B for goods and services procurement.
CSI Divisions: Division 05 - Metals, Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 34 - Transportation, Division 01 - General Requirements
Scope:
The City of Woburn is seeking proposals for the design and construction of a new pedestrian bridge on New Boston Street. This project aims to connect the Anderson RTC with the New Boston Street corridor to support economic development and multimodal growth. The bridge will serve as a critical infrastructure component to create a more walkable, vibrant mixed-use district, reducing car dependency and expanding mobility options for residents and workers.

The project involves preliminary design work followed by construction implementation, funded through a $525,000 Mass Works Program award. The scope includes all necessary engineering, materials, and labor to design and build a pedestrian bridge structure. This will require structural components, foundation work, decking, railings, and integration with existing transportation networks.

Construction activities will involve site preparation, earthwork for foundations and approaches, installation of structural elements (likely including metal components for the bridge framework), and exterior improvements such as walkways and landscaping. The project must comply with all relevant regulations and standards for pedestrian infrastructure and transportation facilities.

The project demands coordination of various trades to ensure proper installation of playground equipment, surfacing materials, and utility connections, all while managing site logistics and compliance with safety standards. Description: This bid seeks a contractor to supply and place sand for beach renourishment to address erosion and maintain coastal areas.
CSI Divisions: Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements
Scope:
The project requires a contractor to supply, deliver, and place approximately 500 tons of screened sand for beach nourishment to address erosion and maintain coastal areas in Brewster, Massachusetts. The sand must be compatible with existing beach sand in terms of grain size, color, and texture, requiring submission of a grain size sieve analysis and Munsell Soil Color documentation. Material must be delivered to and offloaded at 201 Run Hill Road within 48 hours of order (24 hours for emergencies), with all transportation costs included in the bid price.

The contractor must provide manufacturer's certification that materials conform to specifications and may need to allow town inspection of the material source. The initial contract period runs from July 15, 2025 to June 30, 2026, with a one-year renewal option at the town's discretion. The contractor is responsible for ensuring adequate quantities and transportation of materials to the Department of Public Works, with materials subject to town inspection. The project involves physical placement of sand on beaches as part of coastal restoration work, requiring earthmoving equipment and proper material handling capabilities.

The work will be performed at multiple locations throughout the town, requiring flexibility and adherence to safety and quality standards. The contractor must coordinate with town officials, obtain necessary permits, and ensure compliance with local building codes and regulations.

Description: Request for Proposals for a property located at 188 King Street in Hanover, Massachusetts. The RFP was opened on August 1, 2025, and seeks proposals for the development or use of the site.
Scope:
The Request for Proposals (RFP) is for the development or use of a property located at 188 King Street in Hanover, Massachusetts. The property is currently owned by the Town of Hanover and was previously used as a Fire Station. The building was constructed in 1966 and includes a first floor of 1,470 square feet and a basement of 560 square feet, totaling 2,030 square feet of building space. The parcel land area is 6,969 square feet and is zoned Industrial. The RFP seeks proposals for the development or use of this site, which may involve renovation, adaptive reuse, or new construction activities to repurpose the existing structure and land.

Specific details regarding the scope of work, materials, and labor requirements are not provided in the available text snippet. However, without access to the full RFP document, the exact requirements remain unspecified.

The timeline for the RFP includes key dates such as the public release on August 1, 2025, a recommended building tour on August 18, 2025, and deadlines for questions and responses in August and September 2025. Proposals are due by September 12, 2025, with an opening on September 15, 2025. Interested parties are directed to download the RFP and contact the specified town official for addenda and further information.

Project Title: Holden Town Hall Exterior Restoration Project
Description: This project involves the exterior restoration of the Holden Town Hall, including repairs, painting, and other restoration work to preserve the building's facade and structural integrity.
CSI Divisions: Division 03 - Concrete, Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Protection, Division 09 - Finishes, Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and Air Conditioning (HVAC), Division 26 - Electrical
Scope:
This project entails the exterior restoration of the Holden Town Hall, a building registered with the Local Historic District. The work includes demolition of the existing concrete ramp, stair, and guard and handrail system, as well as repair and preparation of exterior siding and trim for new painting. Following demolition, a new concrete ramp and stair will be constructed, and a new guard and handrail system will be installed. The entire exterior façade will be painted, with alternates provided for HVAC and granite step repairs.

Key materials and labor involve concrete for the new ramp and stair, metal or other materials for the guard and handrail system, siding and trim components for repairs, and paint for the façade. The project requires skilled trades such as concrete workers, carpenters, painters, and potentially HVAC and electrical technicians for the alternate scopes. A pre-bid site visit is scheduled to assess conditions, and the contractor must be DCAMM certified in General Building Construction, with filed sub-bids required for painting, HVAC, and electrical work.

The restoration aims to preserve the building's facade and structural integrity, adhering to historic district guidelines. Bidding procedures include submission of sealed bids with security, and the contract may require performance and payment bonds if the proposal exceeds $150,000. The project is procured under Massachusetts General Laws Chapter 149, emphasizing public construction standards.

Materials required include hot mix asphalt for the parking lot and roadway, bituminous and concrete for sidewalks, and related components for curb ramps and crosswalks. Labor involves excavation, grading, paving, concrete placement, striping, and installation of drainage systems. The project requires MassDOT prequalification for highway construction, with an estimated value of $819,450, and includes requirements for bid security, payment bonds, and performance bonds.

The scope focuses on physical construction activities to improve the parking infrastructure, ensuring efficient and safe parking for seniors. All work must comply with relevant regulations and standards, with no asphalt price adjustments included in the bid.

Description: Bid for road repair services. Bid opening is scheduled for March 5, 2026 at 10:00 am.

Description: Bid for crushing reclaim material services. Bid opening is scheduled for March 5, 2026 at 11:00 am.
CSI Divisions: Division 31 - Earthwork
Scope:
The project involves crushing reclaim materials for the Town of Medway, Massachusetts, over a two-year contract with an optional third-year extension. The contractor must provide all labor, services, equipment, machinery, tools, and supplies necessary to perform onsite crushing of an estimated 1,200 cubic yards of material per year. Project Title: Village Street @ Holliston Street Complete Street Improvements
Description: Bid for complete street improvements at the intersection of Village Street and Holliston Street. Bid opening is scheduled for March 19, 2026 at 10:00 am.
CSI Divisions: Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 33 - Utilities, Division 34 - Transportation, Division 02 - Existing Conditions
Scope:
The project involves complete street improvements at the intersection of Village Street and Holliston Street in Medway, Massachusetts. Key work includes realigning Holliston Street with Village Street, installing a shared-use path on the north side of Village Street, and performing curb modifications along the south side of Village Street and at the intersection of Holliston Street and Pine Street. Additionally, Church Street will be dead-ended, and sidewalk reconstruction will occur throughout the project limits. The relocation of the Matondi Square Memorial and its features is also part of the scope.

Materials and labor required encompass earth excavation, full depth reconstruction of roadways, installation of cement concrete sidewalks and granite curbing, and setting up drainage structures and piping. Traffic signage and pavement markings will be installed to enhance safety and navigation. The contractor must furnish all necessary labor, services, materials, equipment, and tools to complete these tasks, ensuring minimal disruption to public traffic and adherence to safety protocols.

Work is scheduled to commence within ten days of the Notice to Proceed, with full completion required by September 18, 2026. The project is subject to prevailing wage rates and requires MassDOT prequalification for highway construction. All activities must comply with local and state regulations, and the contractor is responsible for coordinating police details for traffic safety during construction.

Project Title: RFP - Design-Build Services for the Provincetown Public Safety Facility
Description: The Town of Provincetown is seeking qualified design-build teams to provide comprehensive design and construction services for a new Public Safety Facility to house police, fire, and emergency operations. This project includes site work, architectural design, and all associated construction. Proposals must be submitted through the OpenGov procurement portal.
CSI Divisions: Division 01 - General Requirements, Division 02 - Existing Conditions, Division 03 - Concrete, Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Protection, Division 09 - Finishes
Scope:
The project involves design-build services for a new Public Safety Facility in Provincetown to house police, fire, and emergency operations. This includes comprehensive architectural design and construction services for a complete facility, with all associated site work and building systems.

Key requirements include site preparation, foundation work, structural framing, building envelope installation, interior finishes, and integration of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems. The facility must accommodate multiple public safety functions with appropriate spaces for administration, operations, equipment storage, and emergency response coordination.

Materials and labor will encompass all aspects of commercial construction including concrete work, structural steel or wood framing, roofing, exterior cladding, interior partitions, flooring, ceilings, and specialized finishes for public safety environments. The design-build team must coordinate all trades and ensure compliance with building codes and public safety standards throughout the project lifecycle.

Project Title: IFB - Commercial Street Sidewalk and Drainage Improvements - Phase 2
Description: Invitation for Bids for the reconstruction and improvement of sidewalks, curbing, and drainage systems along a specified section of Commercial Street. Work includes concrete work, asphalt paving, and stormwater management infrastructure installation.
CSI Divisions: Division 03 - Concrete, Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 33 - Utilities
Scope:
This project involves the reconstruction and improvement of sidewalks, curbing, and drainage systems along a specified section of Commercial Street. The work includes concrete installation for new sidewalks and curbs, asphalt paving for related surfaces, and comprehensive stormwater management infrastructure to address drainage needs.

Contractors will be responsible for demolishing existing infrastructure, preparing the site through excavation and grading, installing new concrete sidewalks and curbs to specified dimensions and standards, and implementing asphalt paving where required. The stormwater management component involves installing drainage pipes, catch basins, inlets, and other related infrastructure to properly manage water runoff and prevent flooding or erosion issues.

All work must comply with municipal standards and regulations for public right-of-way improvements. The project requires coordination with existing utilities, traffic management during construction, and proper site restoration upon completion. Materials include concrete, asphalt, drainage pipes, aggregates, and related construction materials, with labor encompassing excavation, concrete placement, paving, pipe installation, and finishing work.

Description: The Town of Salisbury is seeking proposals for the reconstruction of approximately 1,200 linear feet of the primary beachfront boardwalk, including new decking, railings, and structural supports to meet current safety and accessibility standards.
CSI Divisions: Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Composites, Division 03 - Concrete, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 01 - General Requirements
Scope:
The project involves the reconstruction of approximately 1,200 linear feet of the primary beachfront boardwalk. Key requirements include the installation of new decking, railings, and structural supports to meet current safety and accessibility standards. This will likely involve demolition and removal of existing boardwalk components, site preparation, and earthwork to establish a stable foundation.

Materials and labor will focus on constructing durable, weather-resistant structures suitable for a coastal environment. This includes sourcing and installing wood or composite materials for decking and railings, as well as concrete for footings and structural supports. The work must adhere to local building codes and environmental regulations, ensuring minimal disruption to the beachfront area.

Additional tasks may involve grading, drainage improvements, and landscaping to integrate the new boardwalk with the surrounding beachfront. The contractor will be responsible for all phases from design coordination (if applicable) to final inspection, ensuring the boardwalk is safe, accessible, and aesthetically pleasing for public use.

Description: This bid is for catch basin cleaning, repair, or replacement services as part of the Town's stormwater management program for Fiscal Year 2026.
CSI Divisions: Division 33 - Utilities, Division 31 - Earthwork
Scope:
This project involves catch basin cleaning, repair, or replacement services for the Town's stormwater management program in Fiscal Year 2026. The scope includes labor and materials to maintain and improve stormwater infrastructure, focusing on catch basins that collect and direct runoff. Work may involve cleaning out debris and sediment from existing basins, repairing damaged components such as grates or frames, and full replacement of basins that are beyond repair, requiring excavation and installation of new units.

Contractors will need to provide all necessary equipment, personnel, and materials to perform these tasks safely and efficiently, adhering to local regulations and environmental standards. The project aims to ensure proper stormwater drainage and prevent flooding or water quality issues, with activities potentially scheduled across multiple locations within the town. Coordination with town officials and compliance with specified timelines and quality standards will be required.

Project Title: Invitation for Bids: Public Works Garage Roof Replacement
Description: This project involves the complete removal and replacement of the roofing system on the Town's Public Works maintenance garage, including all necessary flashing, insulation, and drainage improvements.
CSI Divisions: Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Protection
Scope: This project involves the complete removal and replacement of the roofing system on the Town's Public Works maintenance garage. The work includes stripping off the existing roof materials down to the deck, followed by the installation of a new roofing system. Key components to be addressed are all necessary flashing to ensure watertight seals around edges, penetrations, and transitions, as well as the installation of insulation to meet thermal performance standards. Additionally, drainage improvements will be made to enhance water runoff and prevent ponding, which may involve modifying or installing new gutters, downspouts, or scuppers. The contractor must provide all labor, materials, equipment, and supervision required for a full roof replacement, ensuring compliance with local building codes and manufacturer specifications. The scope emphasizes a turnkey solution to deliver a durable, weather-resistant roof that protects the garage structure and its contents from moisture and environmental damage.

Project Title: RFP for Playground Equipment and Safety Surfacing Installation
Description: The Town seeks proposals to supply and install new, ADA-compliant playground structures and pour-in-place rubber safety surfacing at Memorial Park, including site preparation and warranty.
CSI Divisions: Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 01 - General Requirements, Division 10 - Specialties, Division 09 - Finishes
Scope:
The project involves the supply and installation of new, ADA-compliant playground structures and pour-in-place rubber safety surfacing at Memorial Park. This includes comprehensive site preparation work to ensure a suitable foundation for the playground equipment and surfacing. The contractor must provide all necessary materials, labor, and equipment to complete the installation, adhering to safety standards and accessibility requirements.

Key tasks include preparing the site by clearing, grading, and compacting the ground to create a stable base. The installation of playground structures involves assembling and securing various components such as slides, swings, climbers, and other play elements, ensuring they meet ADA compliance for accessibility. The pour-in-place rubber safety surfacing must be installed to provide a cushioned fall zone, requiring precise mixing, pouring, and curing processes to achieve a durable and safe surface.

Additionally, the project includes providing warranties for both the playground equipment and the safety surfacing, ensuring long-term performance and maintenance. The contractor is responsible for coordinating all aspects of the work, from initial site assessment to final inspection and cleanup, to deliver a fully functional and safe playground area for public use.

Description: This bid solicits contractors for the repair and replacement of approximately 500 linear feet of damaged sidewalk along Main Street, including concrete work and ADA-compliant ramp installations.
CSI Divisions: Division 03 - Concrete, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements
Scope:
This project involves the repair and replacement of approximately 500 linear feet of damaged sidewalk along Main Street. The work includes concrete demolition, removal of existing damaged sections, and installation of new concrete sidewalks to restore safe pedestrian pathways. Materials required include concrete mix, forms, reinforcement as specified, and finishing tools to ensure a smooth and durable surface.

Additionally, the contractor must install ADA-compliant ramps at designated locations to meet accessibility standards. This involves constructing ramps with proper slopes, landings, and detectable warning surfaces as per regulations. Labor includes site preparation, excavation if needed, concrete pouring, curing, and finishing, along with cleanup and restoration of the work area to its original condition or better.

The project requires adherence to local codes and safety standards, with potential coordination for traffic control or public access during construction. The scope focuses on physical construction tasks, ensuring the sidewalk is functional, safe, and accessible upon completion.

Description: This RFP seeks qualified HVAC contractors to remove the existing heating and cooling system at Tyringham Town Hall and install a new, energy-efficient system meeting specified performance standards.
CSI Divisions: Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and Air Conditioning (HVAC)
Scope:
This project involves the complete replacement of the heating and cooling system at the Tyringham Town Hall. The scope requires qualified HVAC contractors to remove the existing HVAC equipment and install a new, energy-efficient system that meets specified performance standards. The work includes all necessary labor, materials, and coordination for the demolition of old components and the installation of new units, ductwork, controls, and associated infrastructure.

Key tasks encompass the safe disconnection and removal of the current system, preparation of the site for new installations, and the setup of the replacement HVAC system to ensure optimal functionality and energy efficiency. Contractors must adhere to all relevant codes and standards, providing all equipment, tools, and personnel required for a seamless transition. The project aims to enhance the building's climate control capabilities while reducing operational costs through improved energy performance.

Description: This is a bid invitation for the Town Marina Electrical Upgrade Project, seeking qualified vendors for electrical upgrade services at the Wellfleet Marina.
CSI Divisions: Division 26 - Electrical
Scope: This project involves electrical upgrade services at the Wellfleet Marina. The work includes upgrading electrical systems to meet current standards and improve functionality at the marina site. Key activities likely involve installing new electrical panels, wiring, outlets, lighting, and possibly power distribution systems to support marina operations, such as boat slips, docks, and related facilities. Materials required may include electrical conduits, cables, circuit breakers, transformers, and outdoor-rated electrical components suitable for a marine environment. Labor will encompass tasks like demolition of existing electrical systems, trenching for underground wiring, mounting equipment, connecting to power sources, and testing to ensure compliance with safety codes and regulations. The project requires coordination with the Harbormaster's Office and adherence to prevailing wage laws as per Massachusetts General Laws. A pre-bid walk-through is scheduled to allow vendors to assess the site conditions before submitting bids.

Description: This bid is for the supply and installation of new playground structures, safety surfacing, and site work at Cobble Hill Park, including removal of old equipment and compliance with safety standards.
CSI Divisions: Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 13 - Special Construction, Division 02 - Existing Conditions
Scope:
This project involves the supply and installation of new playground structures and safety surfacing at Cobble Hill Park. The work includes removing old playground equipment and preparing the site for new installations, ensuring compliance with safety standards such as CPSC guidelines and ADA accessibility requirements. Materials required include playground equipment sets, safety surfacing materials like engineered wood fiber or rubber tiles, and necessary site amenities such as benches and trash receptacles.

Labor activities encompass demolition and removal of existing structures, grading and leveling the site, installing drainage if needed, and constructing the new playground with proper anchoring and surfacing. The contractor must coordinate with local authorities for inspections and provide all necessary tools, equipment, and safety measures during construction. The project aims to create a safe, accessible, and durable play area for public use, with a focus on quality materials and workmanship to meet long-term durability standards.

Project Title: Wellfleet Marina Piling Repair & Replacement Project
Description: This is a bid invitation for the Wellfleet Marina Piling Repair & Replacement Project, seeking qualified vendors for repair and replacement of pilings at the Wellfleet Marina.
CSI Divisions: Division 35 - Waterway and Marine Construction
Scope:
This project involves the repair and replacement of pilings at the Wellfleet Marina. The work requires qualified vendors to perform structural repairs and installations on marine pilings, which are critical components of the marina's infrastructure. This includes assessing existing pilings, removing damaged or deteriorated ones, and installing new pilings to ensure the marina's stability and safety.

Materials required likely include timber, concrete, or steel pilings, along with associated hardware and fasteners for secure installation. Labor involves skilled workers for tasks such as piling extraction, driving new pilings, and any necessary underwater or shoreline construction work. The project must comply with prevailing wage laws and marine construction standards.

Environmental considerations may be involved due to the waterfront location, requiring adherence to regulations for waterway construction. The scope focuses on physical construction activities, excluding ancillary services like electrical upgrades or general maintenance not directly tied to the piling work. The goal is to restore the marina's structural integrity for continued use by boats and the public.

Project Title: FY26-04 Invitation to Bid (IFB): Worcester Road (Route 31) Sidewalk Construction
Description: This bid solicits contractors for the construction of sidewalks along Worcester Road (Route 31) to improve pedestrian safety and accessibility.
CSI Divisions: Division 03 - Concrete, Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 33 - Utilities
Scope:
This project involves the construction of new sidewalks along Worcester Road (Route 31) between Post Office Place and Birchwood Road to enhance pedestrian safety and accessibility. The primary work includes installing cement concrete sidewalks and pedestrian curb ramps to meet accessibility standards, along with granite curb installation to define the roadway edges.

Additional infrastructure components include the installation of HDPE drainage pipes and precast drainage structures to manage stormwater runoff, ensuring proper water management along the sidewalk corridor. The project also calls for installation of a Rectangular Rapid Flashing Beacon (RRFB) to improve pedestrian crossing safety at designated locations.

All necessary incidental work such as site preparation, grading, excavation, backfilling, and restoration of disturbed areas is included to complete the sidewalk construction. The contractor will be responsible for all materials, labor, equipment, and coordination required to deliver a fully functional sidewalk system that complies with relevant codes and specifications.

Description: This project involves the removal of the Home Brew Dam. The bid period started on February 18, 2026, and proposals are due by 10:00 AM on March 19, 2026.
CSI Divisions: Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 35 - Waterway and Marine Construction, Division 02 - Existing Conditions, Division 13 - Special Construction
Scope:
This project entails the removal of the Home Brew Dam, a physical structure that requires demolition and site restoration work. The scope likely involves dismantling or demolishing the dam components, which may include concrete, masonry, or earthen materials, and managing associated debris. Labor will involve heavy equipment operation, excavation, and potential dewatering or water management during the removal process.

Additionally, the project may include site remediation and restoration of the surrounding area to natural or improved conditions. This could involve earthwork such as grading, soil stabilization, and landscaping to reintegrate the site with the local environment. Materials required may include erosion control measures, sediment barriers, and native plantings for revegetation.

Given the nature of dam removal, there may be specialized construction tasks such as managing water flow, ensuring safety protocols, and complying with environmental regulations. The work is expected to be complex, requiring coordination with utilities, potential temporary structures, and adherence to a strict timeline as indicated by the bid period from February 18, 2026, to March 19, 2026.

Description: This bid is for sewer rehabilitation work, likely involving repairs, lining, or replacement of sewer infrastructure within the Town of Westwood.
CSI Divisions: Division 33 - Utilities, Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 02 - Existing Conditions
Scope:
This project involves sewer rehabilitation work for the Town of Westwood, MA. The scope includes repairs, lining, or replacement of existing sewer infrastructure to restore functionality and extend service life. This likely encompasses activities such as inspecting sewer lines, cleaning and preparing pipes, applying trenchless lining methods or excavating for pipe replacement, and reinstating affected surfaces.

Materials required may include sewer pipes (e.g., PVC, ductile iron), lining materials (e.g., cured-in-place pipe liners), fittings, manholes, and backfill materials. Labor involves skilled workers for excavation, pipe installation, lining application, and site restoration, along with project management and compliance with local regulations.

The work aims to address aging or damaged sewer systems, prevent leaks and blockages, and ensure efficient wastewater management. Contractors must coordinate with town officials, manage traffic control if work is in public rights-of-way, and adhere to environmental and safety standards throughout the rehabilitation process.

Project Title: FY26-03 Invitation to Bid (IFB): Wheeler Road Resurfacing and Related Work
Description: This bid involves resurfacing Wheeler Road and performing related work such as drainage improvements and pavement repairs to enhance road conditions.
CSI Divisions: Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 31 - Earthwork
Scope: This project involves the resurfacing of Wheeler Road in Princeton, covering approximately 19,000 square yards. The work includes reclaiming existing pavement, grading the road surface, compacting the subbase, and applying new asphalt paving. Approximately 4,300 tons of asphalt material will be used to complete the resurfacing. Additionally, related tasks such as drainage improvements and pavement repairs are specified to enhance overall road conditions and durability. The project requires skilled labor for earthwork, material handling, and paving operations to ensure a smooth and long-lasting roadway surface.

Project Title: Uxbridge Wastewater Collection System Rehabilitation Phase 5
Description: This bid is for Phase 5 of the rehabilitation of the Town's wastewater collection system. The bid period started on February 27, 2026, and proposals are due by 9:00 PM on March 13, 2026.
CSI Divisions: Division 33 - Utilities, Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 22 - Plumbing, Division 02 - Existing Conditions, Division 01 - General Requirements
Scope:
This project involves Phase 5 of the rehabilitation of the Town of Uxbridge's wastewater collection system. The work will include assessment, repair, replacement, and improvement of existing wastewater infrastructure components such as pipes, manholes, and related appurtenances. Contractors will need to perform excavation, trenching, and earthwork to access underground systems, followed by installation of new piping materials, rehabilitation of existing structures, and restoration of disturbed areas.

Specific tasks likely include cleaning and televising existing sewer lines to identify defects, spot repairs or full replacement of deteriorated pipe sections using appropriate materials (such as PVC, HDPE, or cured-in-place pipe), rehabilitation of manholes including structural repairs and sealing, and installation of new connections as needed. The project will require coordination with existing utilities, traffic control measures, and compliance with environmental regulations for wastewater systems.

Labor requirements encompass skilled trades such as pipefitters, equipment operators, and laborers for excavation and installation work. Materials will include various pipe diameters and types, manhole components, bedding and backfill materials, and surface restoration materials like asphalt or concrete for pavement replacement. The contractor must also provide all necessary testing, inspection, and documentation to ensure the rehabilitated system meets performance standards and regulatory requirements.

Description: This bid is for annual roadway paving and improvements for Fiscal Year 2027, covering resurfacing, patching, and related road maintenance projects.
CSI Divisions: Division 31 - Earthwork, Division 32 - Exterior Improvements, Division 34 - Transportation
Scope:
This project involves annual roadway paving and improvements for Fiscal Year 2027, focusing on resurfacing, patching, and related road maintenance activities. The work includes physical construction tasks such as milling existing pavement, applying new asphalt or other paving materials, and performing repairs to road surfaces to enhance durability and safety. Labor will encompass site preparation, material handling, equipment operation, and quality control measures to ensure compliance with specifications.

Materials required typically include asphalt mixes, aggregates, patching compounds, and possibly geotextiles or other subgrade stabilization products. The contractor will be responsible for traffic control, erosion prevention, and site cleanup as part of the project execution. This scope covers multiple road segments within the town, requiring coordination with local authorities and adherence to environmental regulations.

The improvements aim to extend the lifespan of roadways, improve ride quality, and address wear and tear from weather and usage. The project may involve minor earthwork for subgrade correction and drainage adjustments to support the new pavement layers. Overall, this is a comprehensive maintenance and construction effort focused on preserving and enhancing the town's transportation infrastructure.

Description: This bid is for traffic marking services, which includes painting lane lines, crosswalks, symbols, and other pavement markings on Town roads.
CSI Divisions: Division 32 - Exterior Improvements
Scope: This project involves traffic marking services for the Town of Westwood, MA. The scope includes painting lane lines, crosswalks, symbols, and other pavement markings on Town roads. The work requires the application of durable traffic paint or thermoplastic materials to existing pavement surfaces to enhance road safety and navigation. Labor and equipment, such as striping machines, stencils, and safety gear, are necessary for precise and efficient marking. The contractor must ensure compliance with local and state regulations for traffic control and material specifications, providing a finished product that withstands weather and traffic wear.

Description: This bid is for crack sealing services to prevent water infiltration and extend the life of paved surfaces on Town roads and parking areas.
CSI Divisions: Division 32 - Exterior Improvements
Scope:
This project involves providing crack sealing services for paved surfaces on Town roads and parking areas. The primary objective is to prevent water infiltration and extend the lifespan of the existing pavement infrastructure. The work requires the application of specialized sealant materials into cracks in the asphalt or concrete surfaces to seal them against moisture and further deterioration.

The contractor will be responsible for all labor, equipment, and materials necessary to perform the crack sealing. This includes surface preparation, such as cleaning and drying the cracks, followed by the application of the sealant using appropriate techniques and tools. The work must be conducted in accordance with industry standards to ensure effective sealing and durability.

The scope covers multiple locations throughout the Town, including public roads and parking areas. The contractor must coordinate with Town officials to schedule work, ensure minimal disruption to traffic and public access, and comply with all safety and environmental regulations. The project aims to maintain the integrity of the pavement and reduce long-term repair costs through proactive maintenance.

Description: This bid is for the design, installation, or upgrade of a hybrid audiovisual room to support both in-person and remote meetings or events.
CSI Divisions: Division 11 - Equipment, Division 26 - Electrical
Scope:
This project involves the design, installation, or upgrade of a hybrid audiovisual room to support both in-person and remote meetings or events. The scope includes the integration of audiovisual equipment such as cameras, microphones, speakers, displays, and control systems to facilitate seamless communication and collaboration. The work requires physical installation, mounting, and configuration of these components within an existing room, ensuring proper functionality for hybrid environments.

Key requirements include the provision and setup of AV hardware, wiring for power and data connections, and potential upgrades to existing infrastructure. The contractor must handle tasks like running cables, installing mounts, and testing systems to ensure reliability and user-friendliness. The project may also involve minor modifications to the room layout or finishes to accommodate the new equipment, but the focus is on the AV systems rather than major construction.

Labor involves skilled technicians for installation, calibration, and training on the new systems. Materials include AV devices, mounting brackets, cables, connectors, and any necessary support hardware. The goal is to create a fully operational hybrid meeting space that meets modern standards for audio and video quality, with minimal disruption to the existing facility.
